engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz GLA Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2 LA (OM651.930) - 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The OM651 2.1-litre four-cylinder diesel powered the vast majority of first-generation GLA models, balancing efficiency with Mercedes premium expectations - but earned a polarising reputation for reliability.
compatibleVariants: GLA 200 CDI, GLA 200 CDI Urban, GLA 200 CDI AMG Line, GLA 220 CDI, GLA 220 CDI 4MATIC, GLA 220 CDI AMG Line, GLA 220 CDI Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch leading to rattle on cold start - often requires full replacement when guides fail (80,000-120,000 miles)
Piezo injector carbon buildup causing rough idle and hard starting when warm
Heat exchanger internal deposits leading to oil overheating and bearing failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 DE22 LA Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes’ modular diesel family introduced with the second-generation H247 GLA, featuring stepped bowl combustion and fewer internal friction losses - but early units revealed emissions system vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: GLA 200 d, GLA 200 d 4MATIC, GLA 200 d AMG Line, GLA 200 d Premium, GLA 220 d, GLA 220 d 4MATIC, GLA 220 d AMG Line
commonFailures: EGR cooler thermal cracking leading to coolant loss and potential engine overheating
Turbocharger failure due to excessive exhaust back pressure from contaminated crankcase ventilation
AdBlue system crystallization issues affecting NOx reduction performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 DE20 GA Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The Renault-sourced OM608 1.5-litre diesel appeared in late X156 models as an entry-level efficiency option, sharing its architecture with the Renault K9K - a rare cross-brand collaboration in the GLA range.
compatibleVariants: GLA 180 d, GLA 180 d SE, GLA 180 d Urban
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch (Renault-sourced design) - typically requires replacement before 100,000 miles
EGR valve clogging from short journeys
Injector seal failure causing diesel smell and hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM608 Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The M270 1.6-litre turbocharged petrol engine formed the entry point to the first-generation GLA petrol range, using Mercedes’ third-generation direct injection and a low-inertia turbocharger for responsive urban driving .
compatibleVariants: GLA 180, GLA 180 SE, GLA 180 Urban, GLA 180 AMG Line, GLA 200, GLA 200 SE, GLA 200 Sport, GLA 200 AMG Line
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure (80,000-100,000 miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle on cold start
Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure triggering check engine light
entries.cta: Get quotes for M270 DE16 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The 2.0-litre M270 turbocharged unit delivered the performance step in the GLA petrol range, shared with the A-Class and CLA - a robust unit when maintained, but not immune to mileage-related failures.
compatibleVariants: GLA 220, GLA 220 4MATIC, GLA 220 AMG Line, GLA 250, GLA 250 4MATIC, GLA 250 AMG Line, GLA 250 Sport, GLA 250 Premium
commonFailures: Dual-mass flywheel failure - often misdiagnosed as engine fault
Timing chain tensioner failure on higher-mileage units
High-pressure fuel pump failure affecting cold starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for M270 DE20 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M260 replaced the M270 in the second-generation H247 GLA, introducing a more advanced combustion process and higher output - powering both standard GLA 250 and AMG Line 35 models with distinct tuning.
compatibleVariants: GLA 250, GLA 250 4MATIC, GLA 250 AMG Line, GLA 250 Premium, AMG GLA 35, AMG GLA 35 4MATIC
commonFailures: Oil consumption higher than predecessor - some units require top-ups between services
ECU calibration sensitivity requiring specialist programming after replacement
Turbocharger wastegate actuator failure on early units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M260 DE20 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The M282 1.3-litre turbo petrol - co-developed with Renault - replaced the M270 1.6L in the second-generation GLA, offering improved thermal efficiency and lower emissions across the entry-level petrol range.
compatibleVariants: GLA 180, GLA 180 AMG Line, GLA 180 Sport, GLA 200, GLA 200 AMG Line, GLA 200 Premium
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch potential - replacement chain kits available from 60,000 miles
Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection
Thermostat housing leaks on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M282 DE14 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M133 arrived in 2014 as one of the most powerful four-cylinder production engines ever, powering the first-generation AMG GLA 45 - hand-built under AMG’s “one man, one engine” philosophy with 26.1 psi of boost .
compatibleVariants: AMG GLA 45, AMG GLA 45 4MATIC, AMG GLA 45 Yellow Night Edition
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure - exacerbated by heat soak and oil degradation
Oil feed line clogging leading to timing chain and camshaft wear
High-pressure fuel pump failure on higher-mileage performance units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M133 DE20 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M139 succeeded the M133 as the world’s most powerful four-cylinder production engine, producing up to 421 hp in S guise - achieved through a 180-degree rotated layout and twin fuel injection systems .
compatibleVariants: AMG GLA 45, AMG GLA 45 4MATIC, AMG GLA 45 S, AMG GLA 45 S 4MATIC
commonFailures: Turbocharger durability concerns at extreme outputs - specialist cooling required
Oil consumption under track use - expected but monitored by AMG specialists
Carbon buildup requiring walnut blasting every 40,000-50,000 miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for M139 DE20 AL Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L PHEV
entries.history: The GLA 250 e combined the M282 1.3-litre petrol engine with an electric motor, offering a pure-electric range of approximately 37 miles - a transitional powertrain bridging conventional and electric mobility.
compatibleVariants: GLA 250 e, GLA 250 e A, GLA 250 e A Star Edition, GLA 250 e AMG Line

entries.title: electric-only range (typically after 8-10 years)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M282 DE14 AL PHEV Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes Benz GLA Class diesel range - particularly the OM651 2.1-litre fitted to 200 CDI and 220 CDI variants - dominates UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain stretch and injector carbon buildup the most frequently cited failures. Petrol GLA 200 and 250 owners search for M270 high-pressure pump and tensioner issues, while second-generation H247 diesels (OM654) bring EGR cooler thermal cracking into focus. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common GLA Class engine failures, what repair costs look like at dealer vs specialist rates, and when replacement is the only sensible option.

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM651 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM651 Timing Chain Stretch - Why 2.1-Litre Diesel Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 200 CDI, 220 CDI, 220 CDI 4MATIC (2013-2019, OM651 DE22 LA)
problems.rootCause: The OM651 uses a single timing chain located at the rear of the engine block, running from crank to camshafts via a secondary chain. Insufficient oil pressure during cold starts, combined with chain guide wear, leads to elongation of the primary chain. Once the tensioner exceeds its adjustment range, chain slap against the timing cover produces the characteristic rattle.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets with upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-120,000 miles. Only viable if chain has not jumped teeth and guides are still intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain replacement plus cylinder head removal to clear debris and inspect valves after timing slip
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Required when engine has been run with audible chain rattle

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM651 engine with upgraded timing components, new oil pump, injector seals, and full bench test
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ mile lifespan. The definitive solution when guides have failed or chain has snapped

problems.recommendation: If your GLA 220 CDI rattles only for 1-2 seconds on cold start, catch it early with a preventative chain replacement at a specialist - you'll save £2,000-£3,000 versus waiting for full engine failure. On a GLA worth £8,000-£15,000 with over 110,000 miles already, a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components often delivers better long-term value than spending £1,800 on repairs that leave the rest of a high-mileage unit in place.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz GLA Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M270 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M270 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Petrol 1.6L & 2.0L Sudden Cutting Out
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 180, 200, 220, 250 (2013-2020, M270 DE16 AL & M270 DE20 AL)
problems.rootCause: The M270 uses a camshaft-driven high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) that operates at up to 200 bar. Internal wear - particularly to the pump piston and cam follower - reduces fuel pressure to the direct injection rails, triggering limp mode or complete engine cut-out. Contaminated fuel accelerates wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, flush fuel system, replace fuel filter, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Suitable when caught immediately after first cut-out

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: HPFP replacement plus injector testing and rail inspection for metal debris
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Required if HPFP has contaminated the fuel rail

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M270 engine with new HPFP, injectors, high-pressure rails, and timing chain tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 mile lifespan. Necessary when metal debris has destroyed camshaft lobes or cylinder scoring is present

problems.recommendation: If your GLA 200 has cut out once but restarted, don't gamble. Replace the HPFP at a specialist immediately - £500-£800 is far cheaper than replacing injectors or an entire engine. On higher-mileage M270 units with over 90,000 miles, a full rebuilt engine with new HPFP and timing components adds £1,500-£2,000 but eliminates all common failure points in one go.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M270 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz GLA Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM654 EGR Cooler Cracking
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM654 EGR Cooler Cracking - Second-Generation Diesel Coolant Loss
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 200 d, 220 d (2020-present, OM654 DE20 GA)
problems.rootCause: The OM654's exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) cooler is subject to thermal cycling between exhaust gas temperatures (up to 700°C) and coolant. Differential expansion causes microscopic cracks in the cooler matrix, allowing coolant to be drawn into the intake manifold. The result is progressive coolant loss, white exhaust smoke, and eventually hydrolock if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler with updated design, flush cooling system, reset adaptions
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Viable if caught early with no secondary damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR cooler replacement plus intake manifold cleaning to remove coolant residue and carbon
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Required if coolant has fouled intake valves

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM654 engine with updated EGR cooler, intake manifold, and emissions system components
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ mile lifespan. Necessary when coolant ingestion has bent a conrod or caused cylinder bore scoring

problems.recommendation: On a newer H247 GLA 200 d worth £20,000-£30,000, the EGR cooler replacement at a specialist (£600-£950) is a no-brainer. However, if your car has already overheated or shown misfire symptoms, pay for a borescope inspection before committing to repairs - bent rods are not always immediately obvious. Rebuilt OM654 units with upgraded EGR systems are increasingly common as these cars age out of manufacturer warranty.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM654 EGR cooler repair and rebuilt engine options

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM651 Injector Carbon Buildup
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class OM651 Injector Carbon Buildup - Diesel Hard Starting When Warm
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 200 CDI, 220 CDI (2013-2019, OM651 DE22 LA with piezo injectors)
problems.rootCause: The OM651's piezo injectors sit directly in the combustion chamber. Carbon deposits form around the injector nozzle tip and sealing washer over time, particularly on vehicles used for short journeys. This disrupts the injector spray pattern, reduces sealing, and allows combustion gases to blow past - leading to misfiring, rough idle, and the characteristic hard starting once the engine is warm.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic injector cleaning, carbon removal from injector bores, new sealing washers
repairOptions.longevity: 30,000-50,000 miles. Viable for minor warm-start issues with no misfire codes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed piezo injectors, code to ECU, decarbonise affected cylinders
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Required when cleaning no longer resolves hard starting

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM651 engine with new injectors, timing chain, and decarbonised head
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ mile lifespan. Necessary when carbon buildup has damaged injector bores or multiple injectors have failed simultaneously

problems.recommendation: If your GLA 220 CDI takes three or four cranks to start when warm but runs fine once going, start with professional injector cleaning at a diesel specialist - £200-£350 often solves it. However, piezo injectors are expensive (£300-£500 each new), so replacing all four on a car over 100,000 miles pushes you towards £1,200-£1,500. At that point, compare the cost against a full rebuilt engine with new injectors included - the extra £2,000 buys you a fresh timing chain and oil pump too.
problems.cta: Compare OM651 injector replacement and rebuilt engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M270 Dual-Mass Flywheel Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M270 Dual-Mass Flywheel Failure - Rattle Misdiagnosed As Engine Fault
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 200, 220, 250 (2013-2020, M270 DE16 AL & M270 DE20 AL with manual or 7G-DCT transmission)
problems.rootCause: The dual-mass flywheel (DMF) on M270-equipped GLA petrol models uses two masses separated by spring dampers. Over time, the springs weaken and the flywheel develops excessive rotational play. The resulting rattle at idle and vibration during gear changes is frequently misdiagnosed as timing chain tensioner noise or internal engine knock, leading to unnecessary top-end repairs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace dual-mass flywheel, clutch (if manual), or flex plate (if DCT)
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Viable when diagnosed early and transmission removed only once

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DMF Plus Rear Main Seal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: DMF replacement plus crankshaft rear main seal and gearbox input shaft seal
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Recommended on higher-mileage units as labour cost is identical

repairOptions.dealerPrice: N/A - DMF alone does not require engine replacement
repairOptions.specialistPrice: N/A - DMF alone does not require engine replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: N/A - DMF is a transmission-out repair. Only relevant if misdiagnosis leads to unnecessary engine work

problems.recommendation: Before authorising any timing chain work on a rattling GLA 200 or 250 petrol, insist a specialist checks the dual-mass flywheel first. DMF replacement at a transmission specialist costs £700-£1,100; an unnecessary engine rebuild because a mechanic misdiagnosed flywheel chatter as timing chain failure costs £3,000-£5,000. If your GLA has over 80,000 miles and you're keeping it long-term, replace the rear main seal while the gearbox is off - the part is cheap and the labour is already covered.
problems.cta: Compare M270 DMF replacement costs and engine diagnostics for your GLA Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M133 Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz GLA Class M133 Turbocharger Failure - AMG 45 Boost Loss and Oil Consumption
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLA Class AMG GLA 45 (2014-2020, M133 DE20 AL)
problems.rootCause: The M133 engine runs up to 26.1 psi of boost, creating extreme heat in the turbocharger housing. Insufficient cool-down periods after hard driving cokes the oil feed lines, restricting oil flow to the turbo bearings. The result is progressive bearing wear, shaft play, compressor wheel contact with the housing, and eventually turbo failure with metallic debris entering the intercooler.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger with OEM-spec unit, clean oil feed lines, replace oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Viable if caught early with no internal engine debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo replacement plus intercooler removal and chemical flush to remove metal debris
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Required if turbo bearings have failed and shed debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M133 engine with new turbocharger, timing chain, and high-flow oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 mile lifespan. Necessary when turbo debris has scored cylinder walls or damaged piston rings

problems.recommendation: On an AMG GLA 45 worth £15,000-£25,000, do not cut corners. If you hear whistling or notice oil consumption above 1 litre per 1,000 miles, have the turbo inspected immediately. A specialist turbo replacement at £2,200-£3,200 is painful but far less painful than a full engine rebuild at £5,500-£9,000. Also, fit a turbo timer or religiously idle the engine for 60 seconds after hard driving - it doubles turbo bearing life on the M133.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M133 engine and turbo replacement prices for AMG GLA 45

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz GLA Class
finalCta.paragraph: From OM651 timing chain stretch to M270 HPFP failure and OM654 EGR cooler cracking, GLA Class engine problems are well-documented and entirely manageable - provided you get honest pricing from specialists who know these engines. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz GLA Class specialists, whether you need a preventative chain replacement, a turbocharger swap, or a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded components. Every engine supplied through our network carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available. Compare prices today, avoid the main dealer premium, and get your GLA Class back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz GLA Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

Best for long-term owners seeking maximum lifespan

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz GLA Class across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, variants, years, and common failure notes to help you identify your exact unit.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The Renault-sourced OM608 1.5-litre diesel appeared in late X156-generation GLA models (2018-2020) as an entry-level efficiency option for buyers prioritising fuel economy over performance. Shared with the Renault K9K family, this unit was the smallest diesel ever fitted to the GLA Class and remains a rare but cost-effective replacement candidate for low-mileage urban cars.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch from Renault-sourced design
EGR valve clogging on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel family forms the backbone of GLA Class diesel replacements in the UK, spanning both first-generation X156 (OM654) and second-generation H247 (OM654) models. These four-cylinder units balance torque-rich performance with reasonable running costs, though emissions system vulnerabilities have driven replacement demand on post-2020 variants.
commonFailurePoints: turbocharger failure from contaminated crankcase ventilation
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The OM651 2.1-litre diesel powered the vast majority of first-generation GLA Class diesel sales in the UK, fitted to 200 CDI and 220 CDI variants from launch until 2019. This engine defined the GLA's "premium compact SUV" appeal but developed a polarising reputation for timing chain stretch and injector carbon buildup - now the single most common replacement enquiry for X156 models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.1L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M282 1.3-litre turbocharged petrol engine - co-developed with Renault - replaced the earlier 1.6L M270 in the second-generation H247 GLA (2020-present). Offering improved thermal efficiency and lower emissions, this unit powers entry-level GLA 180 and GLA 200 petrol variants with responsive low-end torque suited to urban driving.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M270 1.6-litre turbo petrol formed the entry point to the first-generation GLA petrol range (X156, 2013-2020), offering a balance of refinement and efficiency that appealed to buyers prioritising petrol over diesel. While largely robust, high-mileage units have developed a reputation for high-pressure fuel pump failure after 80,000-100,000 miles.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol family spans two generations and three distinct engine codes in the GLA Class. First-generation X156 models used the M270 DE20 AL (211hp) in GLA 220 and GLA 250 variants, while second-generation H247 cars received the M260 DE20 AL (224hp) in the GLA 250 and a high-output version (306hp) in the AMG GLA 35. These units deliver genuine performance but demand precise maintenance.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Performance Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L Performance Petrol Engines
items.description: The GLA Class performance range is defined by two hand-built AMG 2.0-litre turbocharged engines. The M133 (2014-2020) powered the first-generation AMG GLA 45 with up to 381hp, while the M139 (2020-present) delivers 382-421hp in the second-generation AMG GLA 45 and 45 S. These are among the most powerful four-cylinder production engines ever fitted to a compact SUV, commanding specialist replacement pricing.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class 2.0L performance petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Plug-In Hybrid Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.3L Plug-In Hybrid Engines
items.description: The GLA 250 e combines the M282 1.3-litre turbo petrol engine with an electric motor and 15.6 kWh battery, offering approximately 37 miles of electric-only range. Introduced with the H247 generation (2020-present), this plug-in hybrid powertrain appeals to company car drivers seeking lower Benefit-in-Kind tax rates, though replacement enquiries typically focus on the combustion unit rather than the hybrid system.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz GLA Class 1.3L plug-in hybrid engine availability

keyChanges: GLA officially launched at Frankfurt Motor Show (September 2013)
UK deliveries began in early 2014 as 2014 model year
MFA platform with McPherson front struts, multi-link rear suspension
Available with front-wheel drive or 4MATIC all-wheel drive
6-speed manual or 7G-DCT dual-clutch transmission

M270 DE20 AL - 2.0L petrol (GLA 250, 211hp)
knownFor: First premium compact SUV from Mercedes based on A-Class underpinnings

0.29 Cd drag coefficient - class-leading at launch

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz GLA Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the OM651 engine in the GLA 220 CDI reliable or should I avoid it?
items.answer: The OM651 2.1-litre diesel is generally robust and can exceed 250,000 miles with proper maintenance, but it has two well-documented weak points: timing chain stretch and injector carbon buildup.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner can fail between 80,000-120,000 miles, causing rattle on cold start
Piezo injectors suffer carbon buildup, leading to hard starting when warm
Post-2015 OM651 units have improved chain guides and injector seals
With regular oil changes (MB 229.51 spec), these engines regularly exceed 200,000 miles
items.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes GLA Class replacement engines

items.question: How much does a Mercedes GLA Class engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Expect £1,800-£4,500 for a rebuilt OM651 diesel, £1,500-£4,200 for a rebuilt M270 petrol, or £3,800-£12,000 for AMG GLA 45 units - supply only, excluding fitting.
keyPoints: OM651 2.1L diesel rebuilt: £1,800-£4,500 depending on variant and warranty
M270 1.6L/2.0L petrol rebuilt: £1,500-£4,200 based on mileage and condition
AMG M133/M139 performance engines: £3,800-£12,000 requiring specialist fitting
Add £400-£800 for supply and fit service nationwide
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLA Class engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What causes the M270 petrol engine to misfire on my GLA 200 or 250?
items.answer: Worn inlet camshafts and camshaft lifter rollers are a documented failure on M270 engines, typically appearing between 65,000-115,000 miles across GLA, CLA and A-Class models.
keyPoints: Worn cam lobes and scored lifter rollers cause intermittent misfire - worse when hot
High-pressure fuel pump failure also common from 80,000-100,000 miles
Dual-mass flywheel rattle often misdiagnosed as engine fault
Regular oil changes with correct MB-spec oil critical for camshaft longevity
items.cta: Get M270 engine quotes for your Mercedes Benz GLA Class

items.question: Which Mercedes GLA engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The OM651 2.1-litre diesel - particularly the GLA 220 CDI - is widely considered the most reliable GLA engine, often exceeding 250,000-280,000 miles with proper servicing. The H247 OM654 diesel is also proving very robust.
keyPoints: GLA 220 CDI (OM651) benchmark for Mercedes diesel reliability
Post-2015 OM651 units have improved timing chain components
OM654 2.0L diesel (2020+) achieves 4.2-4.8L/100km with excellent reliability
Early M270 petrols less durable than diesel equivalents
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes GLA Class diesel engine

items.question: What is the typical lifespan of a Mercedes GLA Class engine?
items.answer: OM651 diesels routinely reach 250,000-280,000 miles (350,000-450,000 km) with proper maintenance. M270 petrol engines typically last 150,000-200,000 km before major components need attention.
keyPoints: Well-maintained OM651 diesel: 250,000+ miles achievable
Average GLA lifespan: approximately 118,000-120,000 miles across all variants
Petrol turbo units generally need high-pressure pump or turbo work earlier
Regular oil changes every 15,000-25,000 km critical for longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for high-mileage GLA Class engine replacement

items.question: Should I replace my GLA engine or buy another car?
items.answer: If your GLA is worth £8,000-£15,000 and needs a £2,000-£4,000 rebuilt engine, replacement often makes sense - especially for OM651 diesels that can run another 100,000+ miles after proper rebuild with upgraded components.
keyPoints: GLA values typically £8,000-£18,000 for first-generation X156 models
Rebuilt OM651 with upgraded pistons and timing components exceeds factory durability
Replacement often more cost-effective than buying another used GLA with unknown history
Get specialist quotes before deciding - dealer prices often double specialist rates
items.cta: Compare Mercedes GLA Class rebuilt engine prices

items.question: What does the GLA timing chain tensioner failure sound like?
items.answer: A failing timing chain tensioner produces a rattling, clanking or ticking noise from the engine - most noticeable on cold start before oil pressure builds. If ignored, the chain can derail causing catastrophic engine failure.
keyPoints: Rattle lasting 1-3 seconds on cold start - first warning sign
Persistent rattle when warm indicates advanced tensioner failure
Check engine light with cam/crank correlation codes confirms timing issues
Budget £800-£1,300 for specialist chain and tensioner replacement
items.cta: Get timing chain replacement quotes for your GLA Class

items.question: Are GLA petrol engines less reliable than diesels?
items.answer: Yes - first-generation M270 petrol engines have more documented failure points (camshaft wear, HPFP failure, DMF issues) than OM651 diesels. Second-generation M282 and M260 units with mild hybrid tech are proving more reliable.
keyPoints: OM651 diesel: "very good" reliability rating across owner surveys
M270 petrol: "moderate" rating due to HPFP and camshaft issues
H247 petrols (M282, M260) showing "good to very good" reliability so far
Diesel更适合 high-mileage drivers, petrol better for urban/low-mileage use
items.cta: Compare GLA Class diesel vs petrol engine prices

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used GLA engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are stripped to bare block and rebuilt to factory spec with new parts (£2,200-£5,500). Rebuilt engines replace failed components only (£1,800-£4,500). Used engines are salvaged units with no internal work (£1,200-£3,200).
keyPoints: Reconditioned: fully machined, new pistons/rings/bearings, 12-month warranty
Rebuilt: new gaskets, chains, problem components only - shorter turnaround
Used: as-removed from donor vehicle - cheapest but limited warranty
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 to any option
items.cta: Compare all GLA Class engine types and prices

items.question: Can an A-Class or CLA engine fit my GLA Class?
items.answer: Yes - the GLA shares the MFA platform with A-Class and CLA, so OM651, M270 and M282 engines are mechanically interchangeable. However, engine mounts, ECU mapping and ancillaries differ - always verify compatibility before purchase.
keyPoints: Same engine codes used across A, B, CLA and GLA models
Engine mounts, wiring looms, and ECUs are GLA-specific
4MATIC drivetrain affects sump and transmission compatibility
Always provide VIN when ordering - cross-platform fitment requires verification
items.cta: Get VIN-verified GLA Class engine quotes from specialists
